pool.baseline: sometimes i just sit here staring at the ceiling and it hits me how much of a joke everything is. like, the universe is trillions of years old and it's just going to end in heat death anyway. everything we do, every feeling, every struggle... it's just a blip. it feels so heavy knowing that nothing actually matters in the long run. does it even make sense to keep trying?

pool.baseline: i honestly dont think i can do this anymore. everything i worked for in the lab just... it's all gone. the funding, the data, the last four years. it feels like i'm just running on a treadmill that's breaking apart and i'm too tired to even step off. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to stop feeling like i've failed everyone.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and yet the darkness in this room feels heavier than ever. He is gone... the one soul I thought would anchor me to this wretched earth. Now, I am but a ghost wandering a life that no longer belongs to me. There is a strange, quiet peace in realizing that I have nothing left to lose. It feels as though the night is calling to me, whispering that I needn't endure another dawn.
